title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
headline: Fine-Grained Privacy via Predictability: A New Framework Beyond Worst-Case Differential Privacy
tldr: The paper introduces a privacy framework that measures privacy based on predictability, taking into account the attacker's knowledge and a stochastic compromise of the dataset. This approach offers a more nuanced tradeoff between privacy and accuracy compared to traditional worst-case differential privacy.
significance: It provides a more realistic and flexible privacy guarantee that can reduce unnecessary data distortion while still protecting individuals.

title: Caching for Dollars, Not Hits: An Exact Offline Reference for Cloud-Egress Caching and the Crossover That Decides When It Pays
headline: Optimizing Cloud Egress Costs: When Caching Beats Hit Rate for Large Datasets
tldr: This paper provides an exact offline benchmark for cloud‑egress caching, showing that minimizing miss rate is not the right objective when costs are driven by request and byte charges. It identifies the crossover point where caching becomes cost‑effective and offers a reference for evaluating caching strategies.
significance: It redefines caching optimization by focusing on monetary savings rather than hit ratios, guiding practitioners on when to invest in cache infrastructure.

title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
headline: Unveiling How Words Shape Voice: Cross-Attention Attribution in Style-Captioned TTS
tldr: We adapt the DAAM framework to speech diffusion models, enabling fine-grained attribution of how individual words influence acoustic output in style-captioned TTS. This insight helps diagnose failures and improve controllability in expressive speech synthesis.
significance: By revealing the word-level influence on voice characteristics, this work advances transparency and control in expressive TTS systems.

title: VisDom: Sparse Novel View Synthesis with Visible Domain Constraint
headline: VisDom: Enhancing Sparse Novel View Synthesis via Visible Domain Constraints
tldr: VisDom introduces a visible domain constraint to improve 3D reconstruction from few views, reducing floating artifacts and geometry inconsistencies. The method outperforms NeRF and Gaussian Splatting baselines on sparse datasets while maintaining realistic renderings.
significance: By enforcing visible domain consistency, VisDom addresses a key limitation of sparse novel view synthesis, enabling more reliable 3D geometry recovery.

title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
headline: LedgerAgent: Structured State Management for Policy‑Adherent Tool‑Calling Agents in Customer Service Domains
tldr: LedgerAgent introduces a dedicated task state representation that tracks facts, identifiers, constraints, and conditions across turns, enabling policy‑compliant tool calls. By separating state from observations and tool outputs, the agent can enforce domain policies and improve reliability in customer‑service interactions.
significance: This approach bridges the gap between LLMs and structured policy enforcement, offering a scalable framework for real‑time, policy‑aware customer‑service agents.

title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
headline: StylisticBias Reveals How Visual Cues Shape Social Bias in Multimodal LLMs
tldr: The StylisticBias benchmark isolates visual style from identity to test social bias in multimodal large language models. Results show that a handful of visual cues drive most bias, underscoring the need for targeted mitigation.
significance: Understanding which visual cues cause bias enables more effective debiasing of multimodal LLMs.

title: The $B^+ \to K^+ ν\bar ν$ decay as a QCD axion search: comparing reinterpretation approaches
headline: Belle II B+→K+νν Decay Tightens Constraints on Light Axion Models
tldr: Two independent Belle II analyses of B+→K+νν data give axion limits that differ by a factor of four, traced to the choice of kinematic variable space. The study shows that the resolution in the reconstructed di‑neutrino invariant mass q^2 is the key figure of merit.
significance: By pinpointing the source of discrepancy, the paper refines the interpretation of rare B‑decay searches, sharpening the reach for QCD axion and other invisible particles.

title: String Axiverse Enhancement of Superradiant Dark Matter Production
headline: String Axiverse Boosts Superradiant Dark Matter Production from Primordial Black Holes
tldr: We examine how string theory predicts many light axion species that can be emitted by evaporating primordial black holes, enhancing superradiant amplification. The resulting increase in axion production could significantly affect dark matter abundance and observable signatures.
significance: This study links high-energy string theory predictions with astrophysical processes, offering a new avenue to probe dark matter through primordial black hole dynamics.

title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
headline: SARLO-80: Global 80cm Slant SAR–Optical Dataset Benchmark for Multimodal AI
tldr: SARLO-80 provides the first high‑resolution (80 cm) slant‑range SAR–optical paired dataset, preserving complex values and native geometry. This enables training of multimodal foundation models that can learn physically grounded cross‑modal representations for remote‑sensing tasks.
significance: By delivering a large, high‑resolution, geometry‑aware SAR–optical benchmark, SARLO‑80 unlocks the development of more accurate, physics‑aware AI for Earth observation.

title: Transfer-matrix functions for algebraically decaying interactions in variational infinite matrix product states
headline: New Transfer-Matrix Approach Eliminates Surrogate Residuals in Algebraic Interaction iMPS
tldr: The authors present a fixed‑D variational energy formulation that directly incorporates algebraically decaying interactions via transfer‑matrix functions, bypassing the need for finite‑pole surrogate Hamiltonians. This eliminates representation residuals and enhances the accuracy of infinite matrix product state calculations.
significance: By removing the surrogate approximation, the method enables more precise simulations of long‑range interacting quantum systems.

title: HumanScale: Egocentric Human Video Can Outperform Real-Robot Data for Embodied Pretraining
headline: Human-Scale Egocentric Video Beats Real-Robot Data for Embodied AI Pretraining
tldr: Egocentric human videos can provide richer, more scalable data for embodied foundation models than teleoperated robot data, achieving comparable or better performance while reducing cost and complexity. This suggests a shift toward large-scale human video datasets for embodied AI.
significance: This work demonstrates that large-scale human egocentric video can replace costly robot data, accelerating embodied AI development.

title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
headline: FlowEdit: Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation for Frozen Flow-Matching TTS via Associative Memory
tldr: FlowEdit introduces a lightweight, lifelong adaptation framework that updates pronunciation in frozen flow-matching TTS models by learning latent conditioning edits instead of retraining weights. This approach corrects out-of-vocabulary proper noun errors on the fly, preserving zero-shot quality while enabling continuous improvement.
significance: By enabling real-time pronunciation corrections without model retraining, FlowEdit extends the practical utility of state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS for dynamic, real-world applications.

title: Thinking in Boxes: 3D Editing in Real Images Made Easy
headline: 3D Boxes Empower Precise Spatial Editing in Real-World Images for Photographers
tldr: This work introduces a method that uses 3D bounding boxes as explicit transformation specifications, enabling accurate edits of large object motions and camera changes in real images. By integrating structured 3D priors into diffusion-based editing pipelines, the approach achieves more reliable spatial control compared to previous weak 2D-conditioning techniques.
significance: It bridges the gap between 2D image editing and 3D spatial reasoning, offering a practical tool for designers and researchers to perform precise, large-scale edits in realistic scenes.

title: Incorporating physical source parameters into microlensing modeling
headline: Novel Physical-Parameter Sampling Reduces Xallarap Degeneracies for Binary Microlensing Models
tldr: Microlensing event modeling is plagued by degeneracies, especially due to xallarap. Our new method samples source star physical parameters directly, breaking these degeneracies.
significance: By directly sampling source star properties, the approach improves the reliability of microlensing mass estimates and exoplanet detections.

title: On the Renormalization Group Flow of Active Flocks
headline: Renormalization Group Flow in Malthusian Active Flocks: A Toner–Tu Field-Theoretic Analysis
tldr: We develop a renormalization group framework for active flocks described by the Toner–Tu theory, treating density fluctuations as rapidly relaxing variables. Our analysis reveals how momentum non-conservation shapes the scaling behavior and critical properties of polar-ordered active fluids.
significance: This work provides a systematic field-theoretic foundation for understanding the universal scaling laws of active matter systems lacking momentum conservation.

title: Generating Robot Hands from Human Demonstrations
headline: Data-Driven Robot Hand Design from Human Demonstrations Using Deep Learning Techniques
tldr: The authors present a data-driven framework that generates robot hand designs from human demonstrations. By decoupling design and control, the method reduces the combinatorial search space and enables rapid prototyping.
significance: It demonstrates a scalable approach to robot morphology design, accelerating the development of task-specific manipulators.

title: Current World Models Lack a Persistent State Core
headline: World Models Need Persistent State to Truly Understand Physical Reality
tldr: Current AI world models can generate realistic images but lack an evolving internal state. Without persistent state, they cannot simulate object permanence or event continuity beyond observation.
significance: A persistent state core is essential for AI to model real-world dynamics and achieve general intelligence.

title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
headline: Improving Calibration of Mixture-of-Experts Models Under Distribution Shift for Robustness
tldr: This work demonstrates that calibrating individual experts in a mixture-of-experts framework can significantly enhance both accuracy and uncertainty estimates, especially when models face distribution shifts. The authors provide empirical evidence and propose techniques to enforce calibration at the expert level, yielding state‑of‑the‑art performance on benchmark datasets.
significance: By ensuring reliable uncertainty estimates under distribution shift, this study paves the way for safer deployment of MoE models in real‑world applications.

title: SSD: Spatially Speculative Decoding Accelerates Autoregressive Image Generation
headline: Spatially Speculative Decoding Speeds Autoregressive Image Generation by Preserving 2D Locality
tldr: Autoregressive models treat images as 1D token sequences, losing 2D spatial structure and slowing inference. Spatially Speculative Decoding (SSD) restores spatial locality, accelerating generation while maintaining quality.
significance: SSD reduces inference bottlenecks in image generation, enabling faster, high‑resolution visual synthesis.

title: CalTennis: Large Multi-View Tennis Video Dataset and Benchmark of Monocular-to-3D Pose Estimation
headline: CalTennis: 11M-Frame Tennis Dataset for Monocular-to-3D Pose Benchmark
tldr: CalTennis offers 11 million tennis video frames from 40 players, captured with 2–6 synchronized cameras at 60 Hz, making it 10× larger than existing in‑the‑wild motion datasets. The dataset enables robust evaluation of monocular 3D pose estimation models in real‑world sports scenarios.
significance: It provides an unprecedented scale for training and benchmarking 3D pose models in dynamic, real‑world settings.

title: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning
headline: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning: Efficient Uncertainty Quantification for Complex Models
tldr: This work introduces a scalable Bayesian framework that integrates prior-data fitted models with in-context learning, enabling efficient uncertainty estimation across multiple tasks. The approach balances computational tractability with robust generalization, offering a practical alternative to traditional exact inference methods.
significance: It provides a practical, scalable solution for uncertainty quantification in large-scale machine learning systems, bridging the gap between theoretical Bayesian rigor and real-world applicability.

title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
headline: Checkpointing Execution State for Low‑Latency On‑Device LLM Serving and Restore
tldr: This work introduces Execution‑State Capsules, a graph‑bound checkpoint/restore mechanism that captures all runtime state beyond KV caches, enabling low‑latency, small‑batch on‑device LLM inference without sacrificing throughput. It allows interactive AI agents to resume quickly after interruptions while maintaining performance.
significance: It expands efficient prefix reuse beyond KV caches, enabling interactive AI on constrained devices.

title: The FID Lottery: Quantifying Hidden Randomness in Generative-Model Evaluation
headline: Uncovering the FID Lottery: How Randomness Skews Generative Model Scores
tldr: The paper treats FID as a random variable across training and sampling seeds, revealing high variance. It shows that a single FID score can be misleading, urging multiple runs for reliable evaluation.
significance: It exposes the unreliability of single-point FID reporting, prompting a shift toward robust statistical evaluation in generative modeling.

title: Near-Optimal Learning of Local Lindbladians
headline: Efficient Algorithm for Learning Local Lindbladians in Quantum Systems via Classical Shadows
tldr: We present a near‑optimal method to estimate all Hamiltonian and dissipative parameters of a local Lindbladian by running short‑time evolutions and reconstructing Pauli transfer matrices with classical shadows. The algorithm achieves optimal sample complexity and runs in polynomial time, enabling efficient characterization of open quantum systems.
significance: This work provides the first practical, sample‑optimal technique for fully characterizing local open‑system dynamics, a key step toward scalable quantum device verification.

title: On Second-Order Methods for Bilevel Optimization
headline: Second-Order Techniques for Bilevel Optimization: Challenges and Emerging Algorithms in Machine Learning
tldr: Bilevel optimization, essential for modern ML and engineering, faces unresolved issues in finding second-order stationary points, especially when the lower-level problem is strongly convex yet the induced hyperfunction remains nonconvex. Recent research explores cubic regularization and other second-order methods to navigate these challenges, offering promising but still nascent solutions.
significance: Understanding second-order behavior in bilevel problems is crucial for designing efficient algorithms that can reliably optimize complex hierarchical models in AI and engineering.

title: MemoryWAM: Efficient World Action Modeling with Persistent Memory
headline: MemoryWAM: Persistent Memory for Efficient World Action Modeling in Robotics
tldr: MemoryWAM introduces a persistent memory module that enhances world action models by integrating historical observations, enabling more accurate visual foresight and action planning for robotic manipulation. The approach achieves state‑of‑the‑art performance while remaining computationally efficient for real‑time deployment.
significance: By combining persistent memory with visual foresight, MemoryWAM advances robotic manipulation toward more reliable and context‑aware decision making in dynamic environments.

title: TimeProVe: Propose, then Verify for Efficient Long Video Temporal Reasoning in Activities of Daily Living
headline: TimeProVe: Propose-then-Verify for Efficient Long-Video Temporal Reasoning in Daily Activities
tldr: TimeProVe introduces a two-stage approach that first proposes candidate evidence segments and then verifies them, drastically reducing computational load for long video QA. By focusing on sparse, motion-centric cues, it outperforms dense VLM methods while maintaining high accuracy on activities of daily living.
significance: This method enables scalable, real-time reasoning over hours-long videos, opening new possibilities for assistive tech and surveillance.

title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
headline: Assessing Transparency in DiffusionGemma’s Latent Space Reasoning for Generative Models
tldr: We examine whether DiffusionGemma’s reliance on continuous latent space reduces LLM reasoning transparency. By decomposing transparency into variable and structural components, we find that while latent operations obscure intermediate steps, the model still offers interpretable guidance through attention maps and prompt embeddings.
significance: Understanding transparency in diffusion models informs safer deployment and debugging of generative AI.

title: UNIEGO: Proxies as Mediators for Unified Egocentric Video Representation Learning
headline: Unified Egocentric Video Representation via Proxy-Mediated Foundation Models
tldr: UNIEGO proposes using proxy models to fuse multiple viewpoints, modalities, and foundation model outputs into a single egocentric representation. This approach aims to capture richer human action while remaining deployable on wearable devices.
significance: It bridges the gap between limited egocentric perspectives and comprehensive action understanding by leveraging multimodal foundation models.

title: Observation of electroweak production of pairs of Z bosons in proton-proton collisions at 13 TeV
headline: CMS Discovers Electroweak Production of Z Boson Pairs with Two Jets at 13 TeV
tldr: The CMS experiment observed electroweak production of ZZ pairs accompanied by two jets in proton‑proton collisions at 13 TeV, using 138 fb⁻¹ of data. This marks the first evidence of such a process, confirming predictions of the Standard Model.
significance: This observation provides a crucial test of electroweak interactions and validates the Standard Model’s predictions for vector boson scattering.

title: Optimal Deterministic Multicalibration and Omniprediction
headline: Deterministic Multicalibration Achieves Minimax-Optimal Accuracy and Omniprediction for Machine Learning Models
tldr: The authors present a deterministic algorithm that guarantees multicalibration across any set of group weights while achieving the minimax-optimal error rate. This result also yields omniprediction, enabling the model to perform optimally under any loss function.
significance: It resolves a long-standing open problem, showing that deterministic multicalibration can be achieved without sacrificing statistical efficiency.

title: Structuring and Tokenizing Distributed User Interest Context for Generative Recommendation
headline: Structuring and Tokenizing Distributed User Interest Context for Generative Recommendation
tldr: This paper introduces a novel framework for organizing and tokenizing complex user-behavior data to enhance generative recommendation systems. By bridging item semantics with advanced tokenization techniques, it improves the model’s ability to predict next user interactions.
significance: It offers a scalable approach to integrate rich user context into generative recommendation models, potentially boosting recommendation accuracy in real-world applications.

title: From Efficiency to Leakage -- Privacy Backdoor in Federated Language Model Fine-Tuning
headline: Privacy Backdoor Exposed in Federated Language Model Fine‑Tuning with Parameter‑Efficient Adaptation
tldr: The study demonstrates that federated fine‑tuning of language models using adapters can inadvertently leak sensitive information, creating a privacy backdoor. By analyzing the adapter updates, the authors show how malicious participants can embed hidden triggers that compromise data confidentiality.
significance: This work highlights a critical vulnerability in widely adopted federated PEFT workflows, urging the community to develop robust privacy safeguards.

title: Benchmark of quantum algorithms for ground state preparation in the presence of noise
headline: Benchmarking Quantum Ground-State Algorithms Under Noise
tldr: We evaluate cooling, adiabatic, and optimization methods for preparing ground states in noisy quantum systems, deriving scaling laws and validating with simulations. The results guide algorithm selection for realistic quantum hardware.
significance: This study provides critical insights into the robustness of quantum algorithms for practical noisy hardware.

title: Topological Codes Based on Space Groups
headline: Space-Group Codes: Extending Topological Quantum Codes Beyond Translation Invariance for Quantum Error Correction
tldr: Topological stabilizer codes usually rely on translation symmetry, but this work introduces space-group codes that incorporate point group operations. The construction yields CSS codes that maintain topological protection while allowing richer symmetry structures.
significance: This expands the design space of fault-tolerant quantum codes, potentially enabling more flexible hardware implementations.

title: The Token Is a Group Element: On Lie-Algebra Attention over Matrix Lie Groups
headline: Lie‑Algebra Attention: Tokens as Bare Matrix Group Elements
tldr: This paper introduces an attention mechanism where tokens are pure matrix Lie group elements, eliminating feature payloads and learned kernels. The attention score is computed as the closed‑form algebra norm of the relative pose, offering a principled, geometry‑aware alternative to conventional attention.
significance: It provides the first attention framework that treats tokens as fundamental group elements, potentially improving spatial reasoning in geometric deep learning.

title: Zero-Shot Long-Horizon Dexterous Manipulation via Multi-View 3D-Grounded VLM Reasoning
headline: Zero-Shot Dexterous Manipulation via Multi-View 3D-Grounded VLM Reasoning for Long-Horizon Tasks
tldr: We propose a zero-shot framework that grounds language instructions into 3D task plans using multi-view RGB images. By leveraging a vision-language model for 2D keypoint extraction and multi-view fusion, the system generates executable 3D plans without training a policy.
significance: This approach enables flexible, language-driven dexterous manipulation without costly policy training, opening new possibilities for autonomous robotics.

title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
headline: OmniAgent: Real-Time Interactive Video Understanding via Native Active Perception and Cross-Modal Analysis
tldr: OmniAgent introduces a native omni-modal agent that actively selects frames for processing, reducing computational cost while maintaining accuracy. By integrating cross-modal reasoning, it achieves efficient, real-time video understanding without global pre-scanning.
significance: This approach enables scalable, low-latency video analysis for applications ranging from surveillance to autonomous driving.

title: Quantum solitons and their quantum walks in transmon arrays
headline: Exploring Quantum Solitons and Their Walks in Coupled Transmon Arrays
tldr: The study models a linear chain of capacitively coupled transmons as an attractive Bose-Hubbard system, revealing discrete soliton states in the low-energy spectrum. It further analyzes how these solitons propagate as quantum walks, offering insights for quantum simulation with superconducting circuits.
significance: It demonstrates a new platform for simulating nonlinear quantum dynamics, bridging condensed-matter physics and quantum information processing.

title: Beyond the Current Observation: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Controllable Non-Markov Games
headline: RNG-Bench: A New Benchmark for LLMs in Hidden-State Non-Markov Games
tldr: RNG-Bench introduces a suite of controllable non-Markov games that require agents to reconstruct hidden states from partial observations. It enables evaluation of multimodal LLM policies beyond simple recall, providing a more realistic benchmark for closed-loop decision making.
significance: It provides a realistic, open-source benchmark that isolates hidden-state reconstruction, advancing the evaluation of multimodal LLMs in dynamic environments.

title: Constraints on Cosmic Strings from the Curl-Mode CMB Lensing Power Spectrum measured by ACT DR6
headline: ACT DR6 Curl-Mode Lensing Tightens Cosmic String Constraints Using Latest CMB Observations
tldr: The Atacama Cosmology Telescope’s sixth data release provides a curl‑mode lensing power spectrum that probes vector and tensor perturbations from cosmic strings. The analysis sets new upper limits on the string tension, tightening constraints by a factor of two compared to previous studies.
significance: This work demonstrates that CMB curl‑mode measurements can serve as a powerful, independent probe of topological defects in the early universe.

title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
headline: Simulating Human Users with Turing Rewards: A New LLM Benchmark
tldr: This paper introduces a method to train large language models to generate realistic user responses by optimizing a Turing reward, rather than matching a single ground truth. The approach improves user simulation for agent training, personalization evaluation, and social science research.
significance: It offers a more flexible and realistic benchmark for evaluating user simulation models, advancing AI assistants and personalization systems.

title: Fine-scale downflows above flare ribbons captured by Solar Orbiter/EUI
headline: Solar Orbiter Reveals Fine-Scale Downflows Above Flare Ribbons
tldr: Using the Extreme Ultraviolet Imager on Solar Orbiter, scientists captured tiny downflow structures—called riblets—above flare ribbons, offering new insight into energy transport during solar flares. These observations help refine models of magnetic reconnection and chromospheric heating.
significance: This discovery provides direct evidence of small-scale plasma dynamics that influence flare energy deposition, improving our understanding of solar flare mechanisms.

title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
headline: LOCUS: Building a Comprehensive Local Ordinance Corpus for U.S. Legal AI
tldr: The LOCUS project compiles machine-readable local ordinances across the United States, filling a critical gap in legal AI datasets. By aggregating zoning, housing, and public health codes, it enables more accurate AI-driven legal analysis and policy research.
significance: Providing a unified, authoritative source of local ordinances empowers AI models to understand and predict municipal regulations, a previously underrepresented domain.

title: Do as I Do: Dexterous Manipulation Data from Everyday Human Videos
headline: Leveraging Everyday Human Videos to Train Dexterous Robotic Manipulation
tldr: The paper presents a scalable approach to generate training data for dexterous robotic hands by extracting hand-object interactions from monocular RGB human videos. It introduces methods to overcome challenges in pose estimation and embodiment gaps, enabling robots to learn from abundant everyday footage.
significance: This work bridges the gap between human demonstration and robotic execution, opening avenues for cost-effective, large-scale manipulation learning.

title: Sparse-dense flight copy-based interactive mechanism to airline integrated with cruise speed control
headline: Integrated Flight Recovery and Rerouting Using Cruise Speed Control for Airlines
tldr: The paper proposes an integrated approach to flight recovery, aircraft rerouting, and passenger reallocation that preserves their interdependence. It incorporates cruise speed control to improve recovery performance.
significance: It offers a holistic, speed-optimized recovery framework that can reduce operational disruptions and costs.

title: Topological spectral form factor reveals emergent non-Hermitian single-particle $\mathcal{PT}$ transitions from many-body quantum chaos
headline: Spectral Form Factor Uncovers Non-Hermitian PT Transitions in Quantum Chaos
tldr: The study shows that the spectral form factor, a probe of quantum dynamics, can reveal emergent non-Hermitian PT symmetry breaking transitions at the single-particle level arising from many-body chaos. This provides a new non-perturbative tool linking topological defects in partition functions to universal chaotic behavior.
significance: It bridges quantum chaos and non-Hermitian physics, offering a novel diagnostic for phase transitions beyond local observables.

title: The Chandra-Gaia Catalog of Counterparts: Resolving ambiguous Gaia matches to X-ray sources in the Chandra Source Catalog using Machine Learning
headline: Machine Learning Resolves Ambiguous Gaia Matches to Chandra X-ray Sources
tldr: We develop a machine-learning framework that uses photometric and astrometric properties to cross-match Chandra X-ray sources with Gaia DR3 counterparts, outperforming spatial-only methods. The model identifies true matches, flags chance coincidences, and resolves multiple candidate ambiguities, providing a robust training set for future catalogs.
significance: This approach enhances the reliability of multi-wavelength astronomical catalogs, enabling more accurate studies of X-ray emitting objects.

title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
headline: Active Exploration Improves Sample Efficiency for Robust Preference-Based Reinforcement Learning
tldr: The paper proposes a model-based framework that actively guides exploration in preference-based RL, using pairwise behavior comparisons to learn reward models. This approach significantly boosts sample efficiency, especially in early learning stages, by jointly reasoning about exploration and preference signals.
significance: By actively directing exploration, the method addresses the sample inefficiency that hampers current passive preference-based RL, enabling faster and more reliable reward learning.

title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
headline: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation Enhances Reasoning in Large Language Models without Costly Chain-of-Thought Annotations
tldr: The paper introduces a self-distillation framework that conditions on rubric-based quality metrics, enabling large language models to learn reasoning skills without relying on expensive chain-of-thought annotations. Experiments show improved accuracy and robustness compared to traditional supervised distillation and reinforcement learning approaches.
significance: By eliminating the need for costly, noisy chain-of-thought data, this method offers a scalable path to more reliable reasoning in LLMs.

title: Superconductivity from interband coupling to ferroelectric quantum critical fluctuations in two dimensions
headline: Interband Coupling Drives Superconductivity Near 2D Ferroelectric Quantum Criticality
tldr: The study shows that transverse ferroelectric fluctuations, usually weakly coupled to electrons, can enhance superconductivity via interband interactions in two dimensions. This mechanism explains the observed superconducting dome around ferroelectric transitions in several materials.
significance: It resolves the long-standing puzzle of how ferroelectric criticality boosts superconductivity despite suppressed density–density coupling.

title: Future Dynamic 3D Reconstruction: A 3D World Model with Disentangled Ego-Motion
headline: Dynamic 3D Reconstruction: Disentangled Ego-Motion for Accurate Autonomous World Modeling
tldr: The paper introduces FR3D, a 3D world model that separates ego‑motion from environmental dynamics to improve physical consistency in long‑term predictions. By disentangling these components, FR3D reduces artifacts like morphing or vanishing objects, enabling more reliable forecasting for autonomous agents.
significance: FR3D advances autonomous navigation by providing a physically consistent, long‑horizon 3D world model that mitigates common generative model artifacts.

title: Unified Multimodal Autoregressive Modeling with Shared Context-Visual Tokenizer is Key to Unification
headline: Unified Multimodal Autoregressive Model Bridges Visual Tokenization Gap for Integrated Understanding and Generation
tldr: We present UniAR, a unified autoregressive framework that uses a single discrete visual tokenizer to unify multimodal understanding and generation. By eliminating separate visual tokenizers, UniAR achieves more coherent cross-modal representations and improved performance across vision-language tasks.
significance: UniAR demonstrates that a shared visual tokenizer can unify multimodal modeling, simplifying architecture and boosting cross-modal performance.

title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
headline: VERITAS: Real-Time Generator-Verifier Framework for Autonomous Robot Policy Steering and Self-Improvement
tldr: VERITAS introduces a generator-verifier architecture that lets pre-trained generalist robot policies steer themselves at inference time and learn from real-world feedback. By pairing a policy generator with a verifier that evaluates actions, robots can autonomously refine their behavior without external supervision.
significance: This approach enables robots to continuously improve performance in dynamic environments, reducing the need for costly retraining and expanding the applicability of generalist policies.

title: Variable-Width Transformers
headline: Variable-Width Transformers: Uneven Layer Capacity Boosts Language Model Efficiency and Performance
tldr: This study explores allocating variable width across transformer layers, showing that nonuniform capacity improves performance while reducing parameters. Empirical results suggest deeper layers benefit from wider widths, while shallow layers can be narrower without loss.
significance: It demonstrates that tailoring layer width can yield more efficient and powerful language models, challenging the conventional uniform design.

title: PAHSPECS: Spatially Resolved PAH Spectroscopy at cosmic noon with JWST MIRI MRS
headline: JWST MIRI MRS Reveals Spatially Resolved PAH Emission in z~1.1 Galaxies
tldr: Using JWST/MIRI MRS, the PAHSPECS program maps PAH features in star‑forming galaxies at cosmic noon. Forward modeling of data cubes with non‑parametric spatial distributions and PSF convolution recovers detailed PAH emission profiles.
significance: This work demonstrates a new technique for extracting spatially resolved PAH spectra from JWST data, advancing our understanding of galaxy evolution at z~1.

title: MOCHI: Motion Enhancement of Collaborative Human-object Interactions
headline: MOCHI: Enhancing Motion in Collaborative Human-Object Interactions for Real-Time Data Acquisition and Modeling
tldr: MOCHI presents a framework for capturing and enhancing motion data in collaborative human-object interactions, addressing the challenges of dynamic, multi-participant scenarios. By integrating high-fidelity sensing and continuous adjustment modeling, it enables more accurate anticipation and coordination among participants and shared objects.
significance: It advances the ability to model complex collaborative interactions, improving applications in robotics, AR, and human-robot teaming.

title: EventDrive: Event Cameras for Vision-Language Driving Intelligence
headline: Event Cameras Boost Vision‑Language Driving AI with Ultra‑Fast, High‑Dynamic Range Perception
tldr: Event cameras capture asynchronous brightness changes with microsecond latency, providing motion fidelity beyond frame‑based sensors. This enables robust perception in challenging driving scenarios like blur, glare, and rapid motion, enhancing vision‑language models for autonomous driving.
significance: By integrating event data, autonomous systems can achieve more reliable, real‑time understanding of dynamic environments, improving safety and performance.

title: EBench: Elemental Diagnosis of Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies
headline: EBench: A New Benchmark Diagnosing Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies Performance
tldr: EBench introduces a comprehensive simulation benchmark with 26 tasks across five capability dimensions and four generalization dimensions to evaluate generalist mobile manipulation models. The study reveals significant performance gaps among state-of-the-art models such as π0, XVLA, and InternVLA-A1.
significance: EBench provides a nuanced, multi-dimensional evaluation framework that moves beyond simple success rates, enabling deeper insights into the strengths and weaknesses of generalist manipulation policies.

title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
headline: ReproRepo: Scalable Reproducibility Audits via GitHub Issues
tldr: ReproRepo automates reproducibility audits by leveraging GitHub repository issues, reducing manual curation. It offers a scalable benchmark for evaluating LLM agents in reproducing research results.
significance: By streamlining reproducibility checks, ReproRepo accelerates scientific validation and fosters trust in computational research.
